summary: Matched the SysDolphin baselib object allocator by replacing asm/sysdolphin/baselib/objalloc.s with C implementations in src/sysdolphin/baselib/objalloc.c and removing the asm object from obj_files.mk. The PR reconstructs the object heap globals, allocation/free-list behavior, heap/limit checks, initialization/list unlinking, and forget-memory path for HSD_ObjAllocAddFree, HSD_ObjAlloc, HSD_ObjFree, HSD_ObjAllocInit, HSD_ObjSetHeap, and _HSD_ObjAllocForgetMemory.
what_changed: Deleted the 383-line assembly implementation at asm/sysdolphin/baselib/objalloc.s.
Removed $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/objalloc.s.o from TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk so the C object is used instead of the asm object.
Expanded src/sysdolphin/baselib/objalloc.c from only HSD_ObjSetHeap into a full matched implementation of the object allocator.
Replaced the extern assembly-backed objheap label with a named static objheap obj_heap initialized as { 0, 0, -1, -1 }.
Added static HSD_ObjAllocData* alloc_datas to represent the allocator-data linked list formerly stored at lbl_804D76E0.

Implemented HSD_ObjAllocAddFree to assert non-null data, choose between obj_heap-backed allocation and HSD_MemAlloc, align pool starts with (curr + align) & ~align, trim pool_size to available heap space, populate the free-list links, and update free counts.

Implemented HSD_ObjAlloc with num_limit_flag and heap_limit_flag checks, OSCheckHeap/HSD_GetHeap fallback when no custom object heap is set, lazy refill through HSD_ObjAllocAddFree(data, 1), free-list pop, used/free accounting, and peak tracking.
Implemented HSD_ObjFree as a free-list push with used/free count updates.
Added inline removeAll to unlink an HSD_ObjAllocData from alloc_datas before reinitialization.

Implemented HSD_ObjAllocInit with assert_line(0x185, data), memset over sizeof(HSD_ObjAllocData), default limit fields set to -1 where appropriate, align stored as align - 1, size rounded up by alignment mask, and insertion into alloc_datas.
smart_moves: Mapped anonymous labels to meaningful static globals: lbl_80406E48 became obj_heap and lbl_804D76E0 became alloc_datas, making allocator state easier to reason about while preserving the underlying behavior.
Kept the custom heap path and normal heap path separate in HSD_ObjAllocAddFree, matching the original distinction between obj_heap.top != 0 and HSD_MemAlloc fallback.
Preserved the allocator’s alignment idiom by storing align - 1 and using bitmask rounding, which matches the assembly pattern using andc.

Recreated the free-list pool setup with a simple for loop writing next pointers at data->size offsets; this corresponds to the assembly’s repeated/stwx loop behavior without keeping the assembly structure verbatim.
Used assert_line with the original line constants 0xEE and 0x185, preserving source-line-sensitive assert behavior required for matching.
Introduced removeAll as an inline helper for the linked-list unlink logic in HSD_ObjAllocInit, keeping the C readable while still representing the original traversal/update behavior.
decomp_lessons: When replacing baselib assembly, update obj_files.mk at the same time; leaving the asm object in TEXT_O_FILES would continue linking the old implementation or cause duplicate symbols.
For allocator code, identify whether an apparent global is a custom heap descriptor or a linked-list head before naming it; the PR distinguishes obj_heap from alloc_datas.

Heap-limit behavior may depend on both a custom allocator heap and the OS heap. This implementation checks obj_heap.remain when obj_heap.top is set, otherwise calls HSD_GetHeap followed by OSCheckHeap.
Alignment fields in HSD object allocators are mask values, not raw alignment values: HSD_ObjAllocInit stores align - 1 and size is rounded with (size + align_mask) & ~align_mask.

Free-list allocators often store the next pointer inside the freed object memory itself; matching C should use casts such as HSD_ObjAllocLink* or void** carefully rather than introducing separate node allocations.
A tiny unused local can be a matching artifact. HSD_ObjAllocAddFree includes u32 unused; with no functional role visible in the diff, likely retained for codegen/stack matching rather than semantics.
If initialization can be called repeatedly on an existing allocator record, unlink it from the global list before memset; otherwise stale list links can survive or duplicate entries can be created.
naming_conventions: Anonymous static data labels were converted to descriptive lower_snake_case globals: obj_heap and alloc_datas.
The allocator struct type name HSD_ObjAllocData and link type HSD_ObjAllocLink follow the project’s HSD_* type convention.
Public functions preserve the original HSD_Obj* names: HSD_ObjSetHeap, HSD_ObjAllocAddFree, HSD_ObjAlloc, HSD_ObjFree, HSD_ObjAllocInit.
The internal forget-memory function keeps its leading underscore name: _HSD_ObjAllocForgetMemory.
Local names describe allocator arithmetic rather than registers: computed_start, pool_end, pool_size, pool_start.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted matched assembly only after adding the C implementation and removing the asm object from obj_files.mk.
Used static initialized data to replace .data contents: obj_heap = { 0, 0, -1, -1 } matches the former four-word heap label initialization.
Used static BSS-like pointer alloc_datas to replace the former small-BSS allocator list head.
Retained assert_line numeric constants matching the original assembly’s __assert line numbers.
Matched address alignment with bitwise mask expressions equivalent to the assembly andc operations.
Represented the assembly free-list construction loop as a C for loop over num - 1, writing next pointers into each pool element.
Preserved control-flow-sensitive early returns for out-of-heap, zero-sized adjusted pools, allocation failure, num limits, and heap limits.
Called HSD_GetHeap before OSCheckHeap even though only OSCheckHeap’s result is assigned in C, matching the observed call sequence from assembly.

review_feedback.note: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no detailed human rationale or requested change to extract.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify HSD_ObjAllocData fields or objalloc.h to confirm struct layout and field naming.
Search for other baselib allocator matches that use align - 1 and bitmask rounding tactics.
Search for PRs touching HSD_MemAlloc, HSD_GetHeap, or OSCheckHeap interactions with object allocator heap limits.
Look for later cleanups around the unused local in HSD_ObjAllocAddFree or the inline removeAll helper.
